why: Real runtime config protection allows a live-runtime operation when exact real read paths, write paths, and rollback expectations are declared.
The next contour would read, not mutate, the external source path.
Current facts show the external source path clears the primary 401 blocker without any registry mutation.
Registry already points to the current sandbox auth.json.
No different sandbox-local valid auth file is evidenced.
Path equality for explicit auth refs is strict; rebinding without a different target path would not repair the current secret content.
Current risk shapes are split and current facts only prove import necessity.
Combined scope widens rollback without current evidence that registry mutation is required.
Canon prefers the narrower contour when fusion is not necessary.
An exact narrower next contour can be named truthfully.
Exact source path, write surfaces, and rollback expectations are now localizable.
